 {"id":"bd-2lr3","title":"Workstream: Property-based + fuzz testing for core parsers","description":"# Goal
Add property-based and fuzz testing to harden the core “parser/serializer” surfaces where small edge cases can cause correctness or security issues.

# Why
Even with strong unit + conformance coverage, these areas are classic footguns:
- streaming parsers (SSE)
- truncation logic (line/byte budgets)
- JSONL session parsing/serialization
- path normalization and globbing

Property/fuzz tests are an *additional* safety net and a way to systematically discover cases we didn’t think of.

# Scope
- Introduce a small, well-scoped proptest suite for a few high-leverage invariants.
- Optionally add `cargo-fuzz` harnesses if we can keep them reproducible and non-flaky.

# Constraints
- Must not require network.
- Must not introduce brittle golden tests.
- Keep runtime bounded (CI friendly).

# Definition of Done
- We have repeatable generators/invariants for at least SSE + truncation + session JSONL.
- Any discovered edge cases are added to deterministic unit/conformance tests.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:04:45.810310183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:30:05.468373426Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:04:54.561339241Z","close_reason":"Completed: child issues closed; workflows/docs/proptests landed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lr3","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ni","title":"E2E: Rust extension runtime smoke suite (sample) with verbose logs","description":"Background:
- We need an end-to-end validation that sampled extensions run in Rust with real wiring.

Steps:
- Build an E2E script that loads each sampled extension from `docs/extension-sample.json` and runs its scenarios.
- Select runtime tier **generically** based on artifact/manifest (WASM host vs PiJS JS tier); no per-extension branching.
- Capture stdout/stderr, tool outputs, and event hook logs per extension.
- Store logs as artifacts for debugging (per-extension log file).

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs: extension id, scenario id, tool/command/event type.

Acceptance:
- E2E script passes for the full sample and produces logs suitable for triage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

-{"id":"bd-2nng","title":"Enable syntax highlighting in rich_rust for code blocks","description":"Enable syntax highlighting for code blocks in tool output and assistant responses.

## Current State
- rich_rust has 'syntax' feature available but not enabled
- Code blocks from tools (read, bash, grep) show without highlighting
- glamour has optional syntax-highlighting feature also unused

## Implementation
1. Enable 'syntax' feature in Cargo.toml for rich_rust
2. Create helper in PiConsole to detect language and apply highlighting
3. Use Syntax renderable for code output in tool results
4. Integrate with existing truncation logic

## Binary Size Impact
- Adds ~2MB (syntect with embedded syntax definitions)
- Consider: Could use glamour's syntax-highlighting instead (shared dep)

## Language Detection
- File extension mapping (rs->rust, py->python, etc.)
- Shebang detection for scripts
- Fallback to 'text' for unknown

## Test Plan
- Unit test: syntax highlighting produces colored ANSI for known languages
- Manual test: read tool output for .rs file shows Rust syntax colors

## Files to Modify
- Cargo.toml (add syntax feature)
- src/tui.rs (add syntax highlighting method)
- src/tools.rs (use highlighting in read/grep output)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.125262433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.135882462Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nng","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.135805889Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2o4c","title":"normalize_dot_segments: preserve root/prefix and leading ..","description":"Lexical dot-segment normalization in tools/package_manager would pop past root and drop leading ..  {"id":"bd-2ok9","title":"E2E: Message injection + session control with verbose logging","description":"End-to-end test for message injection and session control APIs.

## Scenarios

### 1. Message Injection Flow
- Extension sends pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark'})
- Verify message appears in session JSONL
- Verify display=true shows in TUI output

### 2. User Message Injection
- Extension sends pi.sendUserMessage('test prompt')
- Verify agent turn triggered
- Verify steering modes work (steer interrupts, followUp queues)

### 3. Session Metadata Control
- Set session name via pi.setSessionName()
- Verify name persists in session file
- Set entry label via pi.setLabel()

### 4. Tool Management
- Disable a tool via pi.setActiveTools()
- Verify tool no longer available
- Re-enable and verify restored

### 5. Model Control
- Change model via pi.setModel()
- Verify provider switch
- Adjust thinking level and verify

## JSONL Logging Format
```json
{"ts": "...", "component": "extension_api", "api": "sendMessage", "params": {...}, "result": {...}, "duration_ms": N}
```

## Required Log Events
- message_inject_start
- message_inject_complete
- turn_trigger{source: "extension", delivery: "steer"|"followUp"|"nextTurn"}
- session_metadata_update{field, old_value, new_value}
- tool_state_change{tool, enabled}
- model_change{from, to}

## Artifact Output
- logs/e2e_message_session_{timestamp}.jsonl
- Session JSONL diff before/after

## Files
- tests/e2e/message_session_control.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:20:44.344569665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:20:50.586300575Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ok9","depends_on_id":"bd-1rqs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:20:50.586273514Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ouv","title":"Task: Integrate extension tools into agent ToolRegistry","description":"# Task: Integrate Extension Tools into Agent ToolRegistry

## Objective

After extensions are activated, collect all registered tools and add them to the agent's tool registry so they appear in API calls.

## Background

Extensions register tools via pi.registerTool(). The agent needs to:
1. Query extensions for registered tools after activation
2. Wrap each tool with ExtensionToolWrapper
3. Add wrappers to ToolRegistry
4. Ensure tools are included in provider calls

## Implementation

### In Agent Initialization (src/agent.rs)

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn initialize_extension_tools(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        // Get JS runtime from extension manager
        let runtime = self.extension_manager.runtime();
        
        // Get all registered tools from extensions
        let ext_tool_defs = runtime.get_registered_tools().await?;
        
        // Wrap and register each tool
        for def in ext_tool_defs {
            let wrapper = ExtensionToolWrapper::new(def, runtime.clone());
            self.tool_registry.register(Box::new(wrapper));
            
            tracing::debug!(
                tool = %wrapper.name(),
                "Registered extension tool"
            );
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Call Site

In the agent initialization sequence, after extensions are loaded and activated:

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn new(config: AgentConfig) -> Result<Self> {
        let mut agent = Self {
            // ... fields ...
        };
        
        // Load and activate extensions
        agent.extension_manager.load_extensions().await?;
        agent.extension_manager.activate_all().await?;
        
        // Register extension tools  <-- NEW
        agent.initialize_extension_tools().await?;
        
        Ok(agent)
    }
}
```

## Tool Collection Timing

Extension tools must be collected AFTER activation because:
1. Extension activate() may register additional tools dynamically
2. Tools registered in activate() must be captured
3. activation is when the extension is "ready"

## ToolRegistry Interface

Ensure ToolRegistry supports dynamic registration:

```rust
impl ToolRegistry {
    /// Register a tool dynamically.
    pub fn register(&mut self, tool: Box<dyn Tool>) {
        let name = tool.name().to_string();
        self.tools.insert(name, tool);
    }
    
    /// Get all registered tools for API calls.
    pub fn all_tools(&self) -> Vec<&dyn Tool> {
        self.tools.values().map(|t| t.as_ref()).collect()
    }
}
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No extension tools -> registry unchanged
2. Unit test: One extension tool -> added to registry
3. Unit test: Multiple extension tools -> all added
4. Unit test: Extension tool appears in all_tools()
5. Integration test: Extension tool included in API call

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-254u (get_registered_tools)
- Depends on: bd-3ja5 (ExtensionToolWrapper)
- Part of: bd-1yo9 (Extension Tool Registration feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Extension tools collected after activation
- [ ] Tools wrapped with ExtensionToolWrapper
- [ ] Tools added to ToolRegistry
- [ ] Tools appear in provider API calls
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:01:13.942943830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:01:32.448974904Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ouv","depends_on_id":"bd-254u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:01:31.380649395Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ouv","depends_on_id":"bd-3ja5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:01:32.448946841Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
